Off Calendar Nov-03-2025 Continued To Jan-21-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-31-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT IRINA KRASAVTSEVA's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Defendant Irina Iskander Krasavtseva's Motion to Strike Punitive Damages Claims is OFF CALENDAR,

A party moving to strike a pleading or a part of a pleading must meet and confer with the party who filed the pleading "[b]efore filing [the] motion." (Code of Civil Procedure section 435.5(a).) The meeting must be "in person, by telephone or by video conference." (Ibid.) The meeting must take place at least five days before the date the motion must be filed. (Code of Civil Procedure section 435.5(a)(2.) The moving party must file a declaration with its moving papers demonstrating compliance with these requirements and/or that the opposing party failed to confer in good faith. (Code of Civil Procedure section 435.5(3).)

Defendant here failed to provide any evidence that would support a finding she complied with section 435.5's directive. The parties' meet and confer duties are spelled out clearly and compliance is easy. Non-compliance here is not excused.

Defendants are granted 15 days leave to respond to the FAC.

Defendant is admonished that pleading captions must conform to the established caption for the case. Defendant is not free to use a different caption absent court order.

Defendant is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the above text and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

No proposed order received. Defendant failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Defense' Counsel is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
